items.title: Income and education show distinct links to health and happiness in daily life
items.author: Wendy Berry Mendes
items.content: Newman et al. find that higher education is consistently associated with better health indicators, while higher income correlates with greater well-being, based on a large-scale Ecological Momentary Assessment study.

items.title: The social ladder to wellbeing
items.author: Nataria Joseph
items.content: What aspects of socioeconomic status predict health and happiness? In an ecological momentary assessment study with more than 70,000 people, Newman et al. find that income is linked to happiness but good health is more strongly associated with education.

items.title: Author Correction: A meta-analysis of correction effects in science-relevant misinformation
items.author: Dolores Albarracín
items.content: Author Correction: A meta-analysis of correction effects in science-relevant misinformation

items.title: Representational dynamics during extinction of fear memories in the human brain
items.author: Nikolai Axmacher
items.content: Using intracranial EEG recordings in epilepsy patients, Pacheco-Estefan et al. describe changes in the neural representations of cues and contexts during fear and extinction learning in the human brain.

items.title: The psychophysics of style
items.author: Chaz Firestone
items.content: Drawing on methods from psychophysics across ten experiments, Boger and Firestone examine the cognitive and computational foundations of style perception. Their findings suggest that this capacity is grounded in psychological processes for separating form from content.

items.title: Neural similarity predicts whether strangers become friends
items.author: Carolyn Parkinson
items.content: Shen et al. show that pre-existing neural similarity in strangers predicts future friendship and changes in social distance over time in an emerging social network of MBA students.

items.title: A complex systems view on physical activity with actionable insights for behaviour change
items.author: Maik Bieleke
items.content: Physical inactivity is a major public health concern. This Perspective advocates for a complex systems approach to physical activity, emphasizing dynamic, interdisciplinary strategies to design more effective, theory-informed interventions.

items.title: Quantifying large language model usage in scientific papers
items.author: James Zou
items.content: Liang et al. estimate the prevalence of text modified by large language models in recent scientific papers and preprints, finding widespread use (up to 17.5% of papers in computer science).
